rawText:
In a country where private wealth was eradicated under Mao and has existed again for barely four decades, China now confronts a paradox worthy of its own contradictions: the first generation to get rich under market reforms is beginning to die, triggering a $2.1 trillion intergenerational wealth transfer that threatens to entrench a hereditary elite in a nation still nominally committed to socialist ideals.

The emergence of Xi Jinping's celebrated "nongchaoer" -- young tech entrepreneurs who ride the tide of strategic technologies like artificial intelligence and robotics, typically holding STEM degrees from elite "985" universities -- suggests that the path to wealth in modern China has narrowed dramatically from the freewheeling days when half of the country's 2,000 richest entrepreneurs had no degree at all.

While DeepSeek offers salaries exceeding 1.4 million yuan and the top tenth of software engineers enjoy real wage growth of 8% annually, these gains accrue to a vanishingly small elite concentrated in just three cities -- Beijing's Yizhuang district, Shenzhen, and Hangzhou -- even as the broader economy delivers youth unemployment of 17%, stagnant wages, and a property market that has erased a fifth of household wealth since 2021.

China's cheap-energy advantage, with its power grid adding over 500 gigawatts of capacity last year alone to reach 3,800 gigawatts -- more than double America's -- and data centres securing electricity at roughly half the rate their American counterparts pay, positions the country to potentially dominate the infrastructure layer of artificial intelligence, provided it can overcome the chip shortage imposed by tightening American export restrictions.

The Wahaha inheritance scandal, in which three previously unknown claimants sought more than $2 billion from the estate of beverage tycoon Zong Qinghou -- who died at 79 without a will, as did 91% of the deceased founders of listed firms studied by Tsinghua University researchers -- has gripped the nation and exposed how woefully unprepared China's legal and cultural framework remains for the unprecedented transfer of accumulated private fortunes.

Wealth concentration has accelerated to levels that would alarm the Confucian sages whose maxim "Fear not scarcity, but inequality" Chinese leaders still invoke: the top 1% now hold 30% of national wealth and the top 10% command 68%, up from 16% and 41% respectively three decades ago, while the proportion of citizens who believe hard work pays off has collapsed from 62% in 2004 to just 28%.

Public opinion, hardening against both domestic inequality and foreign rivals, reveals a population increasingly unified in its hawkishness -- with support for military unification with Taiwan rising from 25% to nearly half, and 62% backing costly retaliation against American trade measures -- though high earners, who view American culture more favourably yet paradoxically support Chinese power projection more stridently, suggest that wealth creates its own complex political loyalties.

The Communist Party's response to these tensions has been characteristically contradictory, with Xi Jinping declaring that "common prosperity" is a "major political issue that bears on our party's foundation for rule" while simultaneously refusing to introduce an inheritance tax -- considered and rejected in 1950, 1993, 2013, and again last year -- partly for fear of encouraging capital flight, partly because many party grandees are themselves rich enough to be affected.

State-guided innovation, which channels money from local-government coffers, state-owned enterprises, and national venture-capital funds into favoured sectors while officials "prevent excessive, destructive competition" and tech workers are urged to "cultivate feelings of devotion towards serving the country," has produced globally competitive firms in electric vehicles, renewables, and AI, yet big industrial firms have shed 23 million jobs since 2014, suggesting that the benefits of this techno-nationalist strategy may never trickle down to the ordinary worker.

The sentiment of a young woman in Guizhou -- who jokes with friends that the real distinction between successful people and everyone else lies not in exam scores or job titles but in "whose amniotic fluid they were formed in" -- captures a generational disillusionment so profound that it has spawned an entire vocabulary of resignation, from "lying flat" to "full-time children" to the bitter arithmetic of involution, where effort might take you from 1 to 1.2 in a system that once promised leaps from 1 to 100.

overview.title: New Money, Old Problems: Wealth and Power in Modern China

sentences.original: In a country where private wealth was eradicated under Mao and has existed again for barely four decades, China now confronts a paradox worthy of its own contradictions: the first generation to get rich under market reforms is beginning to die, triggering a $2.1 trillion intergenerational wealth transfer that threatens to entrench a hereditary elite in a nation still nominally committed to socialist ideals.
sentences.translation: 마오쩌둥 치하에서 사유 재산이 근절되었다가 겨우 40년 만에 다시 존재하게 된 나라에서, 중국은 이제 그 자체의 모순에 걸맞은 역설에 직면하고 있다. 시장 개혁 아래 부를 축적한 첫 세대가 세상을 떠나기 시작하면서 2조 1천억 달러 규모의 세대 간 부의 이전이 촉발되고 있는데, 이는 여전히 명목상 사회주의 이념에 헌신하는 국가에서 세습 엘리트를 공고히 할 위험이 있다.

grammarElements.text: In a country where private wealth was eradicated under Mao
grammarElements.label: 장소 부사구 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: China now confronts a paradox
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: worthy of its own contradictions
grammarElements.label: 형용사구 후치수식

grammarElements.text: triggering a $2.1 trillion intergenerational wealth transfer
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

grammarElements.text: that threatens to entrench a hereditary elite
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: The revolution eradicated private ownership across the entire country.
Tax loopholes entrench advantages for the already wealthy.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것이 특정한 수준이나 성격에 부합할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: In a [context] where [historical background], [entity] now confronts [paradox/challenge]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 배경을 장소절로 제시한 뒤 현재의 역설적 상황을 드러내는 도입 패턴
examples: In a region where democracy was suppressed for decades, voters now confront the paradox of too many choices.
In an industry where profits were guaranteed, firms now confront the challenge of genuine competition.

drillQuestions.question: In a country where private wealth was ______ under Mao, China now confronts a paradox.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:)이 역설의 구체적 내용을 도입한다. 사회주의 이념과 세습 엘리트라는 양립 불가능한 요소의 충돌이 기사 전체의 핵심 긴장을 형성한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In a country where private wealth was eradicated under Mao
koreanAlignment.ko: 마오쩌둥 치하에서 사유 재산이 근절된 나라에서

koreanAlignment.en: China now confronts a paradox worthy of its own contradictions
koreanAlignment.ko: 중국은 그 자체의 모순에 걸맞은 역설에 직면하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: triggering a $2.1 trillion intergenerational wealth transfer
koreanAlignment.ko: 2조 1천억 달러 규모의 세대 간 부의 이전이 촉발되고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: in a nation still nominally committed to socialist ideals
koreanAlignment.ko: 여전히 명목상 사회주의 이념에 헌신하는 국가에서

sentences.original: The emergence of Xi Jinping's celebrated "nongchaoer" -- young tech entrepreneurs who ride the tide of strategic technologies like artificial intelligence and robotics, typically holding STEM degrees from elite "985" universities -- suggests that the path to wealth in modern China has narrowed dramatically from the freewheeling days when half of the country's 2,000 richest entrepreneurs had no degree at all.
sentences.translation: 시진핑이 칭송하는 '농차오얼(弄潮儿)', 즉 인공지능과 로봇공학 같은 전략 기술의 물결을 타는 젊은 기술 창업가들로 대개 엘리트 '985' 대학의 이공계 학위를 소지한 이들의 등장은, 2천 명의 최고 부호 중 절반이 학위조차 없던 자유분방한 시절과 비교해 현대 중국에서 부로 가는 길이 극적으로 좁아졌음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: The emergence of Xi Jinping's celebrated "nongchaoer"
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: young tech entrepreneurs who ride the tide of strategic technologies
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격어구 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: suggests that
grammarElements.label: 본동사 + that절

grammarElements.text: the path to wealth in modern China has narrowed dramatically
grammarElements.label: that절 내 주절

grammarElements.text: from the freewheeling days when half of the country's 2,000 richest entrepreneurs had no degree at all
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The freewheeling era of internet startups gave way to tighter regulation.

expressions.usage: 시대적 트렌드나 변화의 흐름을 잘 활용하는 것을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: The emergence of [new phenomenon] suggests that [path/system] has narrowed from [past openness]
patterns.explanation: 새로운 현상의 등장이 기회의 축소를 보여준다는 분석 패턴
examples: The emergence of AI-driven hiring suggests that the path to employment has narrowed from the days of walk-in interviews.
The emergence of mega-funds suggests that the path to venture success has narrowed from the era of garage startups.

drillQuestions.question: The emergence of "nongchaoer" suggests that the path to wealth has ______ dramatically.

sentences.notes: nongchaoer(弄潮儿)는 '파도를 부리는 자'라는 뜻의 중국어 표현이다. 과거의 자유분방한 시대와 현재의 학벌 중심 엘리트 경로를 대비시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: The emergence of Xi Jinping's celebrated "nongchaoer"
young tech entrepreneurs who ride the tide of strategic technologies
koreanAlignment.ko: 전략 기술의 물결을 타는 젊은 기술 창업가들

koreanAlignment.en: the path to wealth in modern China has narrowed dramatically
koreanAlignment.ko: 현대 중국에서 부로 가는 길이 극적으로 좁아졌다

koreanAlignment.en: the freewheeling days

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: historical contrast (과거와 현재의 기회 구조 대비)

sentences.original: While DeepSeek offers salaries exceeding 1.4 million yuan and the top tenth of software engineers enjoy real wage growth of 8% annually, these gains accrue to a vanishingly small elite concentrated in just three cities -- Beijing's Yizhuang district, Shenzhen, and Hangzhou -- even as the broader economy delivers youth unemployment of 17%, stagnant wages, and a property market that has erased a fifth of household wealth since 2021.
sentences.translation: 딥시크가 140만 위안을 초과하는 연봉을 제시하고 상위 10%의 소프트웨어 엔지니어들이 연간 8%의 실질 임금 상승을 누리는 반면, 이러한 이득은 베이징 이좡구, 선전, 항저우 단 세 도시에 집중된 극소수 엘리트에게만 돌아가며, 더 넓은 경제는 17%의 청년 실업률과 정체된 임금, 그리고 2021년 이후 가계 자산의 5분의 1을 지워버린 부동산 시장을 안겨주고 있다.

grammarElements.text: While DeepSeek offers salaries exceeding 1.4 million yuan
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (While)

grammarElements.text: these gains accrue to a vanishingly small elite
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: concentrated in just three cities
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 후치수식

grammarElements.text: even as the broader economy delivers
grammarElements.label: even as 대비절

vocabulary.example: The benefits of automation accrue mainly to shareholders, not workers.
The chance of success is vanishingly small without additional funding.

expressions.usage: 두 가지 대비되는 상황이 동시에 진행됨을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: While [impressive gains for few], these gains accrue to [tiny group] even as [broader population suffers]
patterns.explanation: 소수의 성과와 다수의 고통을 대비시키는 불평등 묘사 패턴
examples: While hedge funds post record returns, these gains accrue to the top 0.1% even as median incomes stagnate.
While Silicon Valley celebrates billion-dollar exits, these gains accrue to a narrow class even as housing costs crush the middle class.

drillQuestions.question: These gains ______ to a vanishingly small elite concentrated in just three cities.

sentences.notes: While절과 even as절의 이중 대비 구조가 기술 엘리트의 풍요와 나머지 인구의 고통을 극적으로 병치시킨다. vanishingly small이라는 부사-형용사 결합이 엘리트의 극소수성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: While DeepSeek offers salaries exceeding 1.4 million yuan
koreanAlignment.ko: 딥시크가 140만 위안을 초과하는 연봉을 제시하는 반면

koreanAlignment.en: these gains accrue to a vanishingly small elite
even as the broader economy delivers youth unemployment of 17%
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 넓은 경제는 17%의 청년 실업률을 안겨주고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: a property market that has erased a fifth of household wealth

pronunciationNotes: accrue: /əˈkruː/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세, crew와 같은 발음

sentences.original: China's cheap-energy advantage, with its power grid adding over 500 gigawatts of capacity last year alone to reach 3,800 gigawatts -- more than double America's -- and data centres securing electricity at roughly half the rate their American counterparts pay, positions the country to potentially dominate the infrastructure layer of artificial intelligence, provided it can overcome the chip shortage imposed by tightening American export restrictions.
sentences.translation: 지난해에만 500기가와트 이상의 용량을 추가해 3,800기가와트에 도달한 전력망(미국의 두 배 이상)과 미국 경쟁사들이 지불하는 요금의 대략 절반으로 전력을 확보하는 데이터센터가 뒷받침하는 중국의 저가 에너지 이점은, 미국의 수출 규제 강화가 부과한 반도체 부족을 극복할 수 있다는 전제 하에, 인공지능의 인프라 계층을 잠재적으로 지배할 수 있는 위치에 중국을 놓아준다.

grammarElements.text: China's cheap-energy advantage
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: with its power grid adding over 500 gigawatts
grammarElements.label: with + 독립분사구문

grammarElements.text: positions the country to potentially dominate
grammarElements.label: 본동사 + 목적격보어

grammarElements.text: provided it can overcome the chip shortage
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (provided)

vocabulary.example: Chinese data centres pay far less for electricity than their American counterparts.

expressions.usage: 특정 조건이 충족될 때만 성립하는 상황을 제시할 때 사용하는 격식체 표현

patterns.pattern: [Advantage], with [supporting evidence], positions [entity] to [potential outcome], provided [condition]
patterns.explanation: 이점을 근거와 함께 제시한 뒤 잠재적 결과와 전제 조건을 붙이는 분석 패턴
examples: India's demographic dividend, with 600 million people under 25, positions the country to lead global manufacturing, provided it can reform its education system.
The firm's cash reserves, with $50 billion on the balance sheet, position it to acquire competitors, provided regulators approve.

drillQuestions.question: China's cheap-energy advantage ______ the country to potentially dominate the infrastructure layer of AI.

sentences.notes: with 독립분사구문이 구체적인 수치를 제공하며 주장을 뒷받침한다. provided 조건절이 낙관론에 중요한 단서를 붙여 균형 잡힌 분석을 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: China's cheap-energy advantage
with its power grid adding over 500 gigawatts of capacity
koreanAlignment.ko: 전력망이 500기가와트 이상의 용량을 추가한 것과 함께

koreanAlignment.en: positions the country to potentially dominate
koreanAlignment.ko: 잠재적으로 지배할 수 있는 위치에 놓아준다

koreanAlignment.en: provided it can overcome the chip shortage
koreanAlignment.ko: 반도체 부족을 극복할 수 있다는 전제 하에

pronunciationNotes: gigawatts: /ˈɡɪɡəwɒts/ — gi는 /ɡɪ/로, 영화 '백 투 더 퓨처'의 /dʒɪɡə/가 아님

sentences.original: The Wahaha inheritance scandal, in which three previously unknown claimants sought more than $2 billion from the estate of beverage tycoon Zong Qinghou -- who died at 79 without a will, as did 91% of the deceased founders of listed firms studied by Tsinghua University researchers -- has gripped the nation and exposed how woefully unprepared China's legal and cultural framework remains for the unprecedented transfer of accumulated private fortunes.
sentences.translation: 이전에는 알려지지 않았던 세 명의 청구인이 음료 재벌 종칭허우의 유산에서 20억 달러 이상을 요구한 와하하 상속 스캔들은, 종칭허우가 79세에 유언장 없이 사망했는데 칭화대학교 연구진이 조사한 상장기업 사망 창업자의 91%도 마찬가지였다는 점에서, 전국을 사로잡았으며 축적된 사유 재산의 전례 없는 이전에 중국의 법적·문화적 체계가 얼마나 한심할 정도로 준비되지 않았는지를 드러냈다.

grammarElements.text: The Wahaha inheritance scandal
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: in which three previously unknown claimants sought more than $2 billion
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (전치사 + which)

grammarElements.text: has gripped the nation and exposed
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 병렬 동사

grammarElements.text: how woefully unprepared China's legal and cultural framework remains
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (how + 형용사)

vocabulary.example: The beverage tycoon built his empire from a single factory in Hangzhou.
Several claimants emerged after the billionaire died without a will.

expressions.meaning: 전국을 사로잡다, 온 나라의 관심을 끌다
expressions.usage: 특정 사건이나 이슈가 대중의 폭넓은 관심을 집중시킬 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Scandal/Event] has gripped [audience] and exposed how [adverb] [unprepared/inadequate] [system] remains for [challenge]
patterns.explanation: 특정 사건이 시스템의 미비함을 노출시켰다는 것을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The data breach has gripped the industry and exposed how dangerously outdated its cybersecurity standards remain.
The flood has gripped the region and exposed how criminally neglected its infrastructure remains.

drillQuestions.question: The scandal has gripped the nation and exposed how ______ unprepared China's legal framework remains.

sentences.notes: 대시 안의 통계(91%)가 와하하 사례를 개별 스캔들이 아닌 체계적 문제로 확장시킨다. as did 91%라는 도치 구문에 주목할 것.

koreanAlignment.en: The Wahaha inheritance scandal
three previously unknown claimants sought more than $2 billion
koreanAlignment.ko: 이전에 알려지지 않았던 세 명의 청구인이 20억 달러 이상을 요구했다

koreanAlignment.en: has gripped the nation
how woefully unprepared China's legal and cultural framework remains
koreanAlignment.ko: 중국의 법적·문화적 체계가 얼마나 준비되지 않았는지

pronunciationNotes: Wahaha: /wɑːhɑːhɑː/ — 중국어 음역, 세 음절 모두 /ɑː/

sentences.original: Wealth concentration has accelerated to levels that would alarm the Confucian sages whose maxim "Fear not scarcity, but inequality" Chinese leaders still invoke: the top 1% now hold 30% of national wealth and the top 10% command 68%, up from 16% and 41% respectively three decades ago, while the proportion of citizens who believe hard work pays off has collapsed from 62% in 2004 to just 28%.
sentences.translation: 부의 집중은 '부족함을 두려워하지 말고 불평등을 두려워하라'는 격언을 남긴 유교 성현들마저 경악시킬 수준까지 가속화되었으며 중국 지도자들은 여전히 이 격언을 인용한다. 상위 1%가 국부의 30%, 상위 10%가 68%를 차지하는데 30년 전에는 각각 16%와 41%였고, 한편으로 열심히 일하면 보상받는다고 믿는 시민의 비율은 2004년 62%에서 겨우 28%로 폭락했다.

grammarElements.text: Wealth concentration has accelerated to levels
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: that would alarm the Confucian sages
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (가정법 would)

grammarElements.text: whose maxim ... Chinese leaders still invoke
grammarElements.label: 소유격 관계절

grammarElements.text: the top 1% now hold 30% ... and the top 10% command 68%
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 구체적 설명

vocabulary.example: Politicians still invoke Confucian values to justify social policies.

expressions.usage: 노력이나 투자가 결국 좋은 결과를 낳을 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Trend] has accelerated to levels that would alarm [authority figure]: [specific data]
patterns.explanation: 추세가 권위 있는 기준을 넘어섰음을 수치로 뒷받침하는 패턴
examples: Income inequality has accelerated to levels that would alarm the Founding Fathers: the top 0.1% now hold as much wealth as the bottom 90%.
Deforestation has accelerated to levels that would alarm early conservationists: 80% of the original rainforest is now gone.

drillQuestions.question: Wealth concentration has accelerated to levels that would ______ the Confucian sages.

sentences.notes: 유교 성현의 격언(不患寡而患不均)을 영어로 풀어 인용하면서 현대 중국의 불평등을 비판한다. 콜론 뒤의 구체적 수치가 추상적 주장에 설득력을 더한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Wealth concentration has accelerated to levels
that would alarm the Confucian sages
the top 1% now hold 30% of national wealth
the proportion of citizens who believe hard work pays off has collapsed
koreanAlignment.ko: 열심히 일하면 보상받는다고 믿는 시민의 비율이 폭락했다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: appeal to authority (유교 성현의 권위 인용)

sentences.original: Public opinion, hardening against both domestic inequality and foreign rivals, reveals a population increasingly unified in its hawkishness -- with support for military unification with Taiwan rising from 25% to nearly half, and 62% backing costly retaliation against American trade measures -- though high earners, who view American culture more favourably yet paradoxically support Chinese power projection more stridently, suggest that wealth creates its own complex political loyalties.
sentences.translation: 국내 불평등과 외국 경쟁국 모두에 대해 경직되고 있는 여론은 점점 더 강경론으로 통합되는 인구를 드러내는데, 대만과의 군사적 통일 지지가 25%에서 거의 절반으로 상승했고 62%가 미국 무역 조치에 대한 비용이 많이 드는 보복을 지지하고 있다. 다만, 미국 문화는 더 호의적으로 보면서도 역설적으로 중국의 힘의 투사를 더 강하게 지지하는 고소득자들은, 부가 그 자체의 복잡한 정치적 충성심을 만들어냄을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: Public opinion, hardening against both domestic inequality and foreign rivals
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 분사구문

grammarElements.text: reveals a population increasingly unified in its hawkishness
grammarElements.label: 본동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: though high earners ... suggest that
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (though)

grammarElements.text: wealth creates its own complex political loyalties
grammarElements.label: that절 (결론)

vocabulary.example: Rising hawkishness among young voters has surprised foreign policy analysts.
The opposition stridently opposed the trade deal despite its economic benefits.

expressions.usage: 한 국가가 자국 영토 밖에서 군사적·정치적 영향력을 행사하는 것을 가리킴

patterns.pattern: [Public opinion], hardening against [targets], reveals [trend] -- though [exception] suggests that [complexity]
patterns.explanation: 전반적 여론 경향을 제시한 뒤 예외적 집단으로 복잡성을 드러내는 패턴
examples: Voter sentiment, hardening against immigration, reveals broad support for restrictions -- though younger respondents suggest that generational attitudes are more nuanced.
Market sentiment, hardening against tech stocks, reveals widespread caution -- though institutional buyers suggest that professional opinion diverges from retail panic.

drillQuestions.question: Public opinion, ______ against both domestic inequality and foreign rivals, reveals a population increasingly unified.

sentences.notes: paradoxically가 고소득자의 모순적 태도를 포착한다. 미국 문화에 호의적이면서 동시에 중국의 힘의 투사를 더 강하게 지지하는 이중성이 부가 만들어내는 복잡한 충성심의 핵심이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Public opinion, hardening against both domestic inequality and foreign rivals
koreanAlignment.ko: 국내 불평등과 외국 경쟁국 모두에 대해 경직되는 여론

koreanAlignment.en: reveals a population increasingly unified in its hawkishness
koreanAlignment.ko: 점점 더 강경론으로 통합되는 인구를 드러낸다

koreanAlignment.en: yet paradoxically support Chinese power projection more stridently
koreanAlignment.ko: 역설적으로 중국의 힘의 투사를 더 강하게 지지한다

koreanAlignment.en: wealth creates its own complex political loyalties
koreanAlignment.ko: 부가 그 자체의 복잡한 정치적 충성심을 만들어낸다

sentences.original: The Communist Party's response to these tensions has been characteristically contradictory, with Xi Jinping declaring that "common prosperity" is a "major political issue that bears on our party's foundation for rule" while simultaneously refusing to introduce an inheritance tax -- considered and rejected in 1950, 1993, 2013, and again last year -- partly for fear of encouraging capital flight, partly because many party grandees are themselves rich enough to be affected.
sentences.translation: 이러한 긴장에 대한 공산당의 대응은 전형적으로 모순적이었는데, 시진핑은 '공동부유'가 '우리 당의 집권 기반과 관련된 주요 정치적 사안'이라고 선언하면서도 동시에 상속세 도입을 거부하고 있다. 상속세는 1950년, 1993년, 2013년, 그리고 지난해에도 검토 후 거부되었는데, 부분적으로는 자본 유출을 부추길 우려 때문이고 부분적으로는 많은 당 원로들이 직접 영향을 받을 만큼 부유하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The Communist Party's response to these tensions
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has been characteristically contradictory
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 + 보어

grammarElements.text: with Xi Jinping declaring ... while simultaneously refusing
grammarElements.label: with 독립분사구문 (동시 대비)

grammarElements.text: partly for fear of ... partly because ...
grammarElements.label: 이중 이유 부사구

vocabulary.example: The government's contradictory signals confused both markets and citizens.

expressions.usage: 어떤 사안이 더 큰 문제나 기반에 영향을 미칠 때 사용하는 격식체 표현

patterns.pattern: [Entity]'s response has been characteristically contradictory, with [action A] while simultaneously [action B]
patterns.explanation: 주체의 대응이 본질적으로 모순됨을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The Fed's response has been characteristically contradictory, with officials warning about inflation while simultaneously expanding the balance sheet.
The EU's response has been characteristically contradictory, with leaders calling for unity while simultaneously protecting national interests.

drillQuestions.question: The Communist Party's response has been characteristically ______, with Xi Jinping declaring common prosperity while refusing an inheritance tax.

sentences.notes: characteristically라는 부사가 모순이 이번만의 실수가 아닌 당의 본질적 특성임을 암시한다. 상속세 거부의 이중 이유(partly ... partly)가 이념과 이해관계의 충돌을 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: The Communist Party's response to these tensions
has been characteristically contradictory
while simultaneously refusing to introduce an inheritance tax
many party grandees are themselves rich enough to be affected
koreanAlignment.ko: 많은 당 원로들이 직접 영향을 받을 만큼 부유하다

sentences.original: State-guided innovation, which channels money from local-government coffers, state-owned enterprises, and national venture-capital funds into favoured sectors while officials "prevent excessive, destructive competition" and tech workers are urged to "cultivate feelings of devotion towards serving the country," has produced globally competitive firms in electric vehicles, renewables, and AI, yet big industrial firms have shed 23 million jobs since 2014, suggesting that the benefits of this techno-nationalist strategy may never trickle down to the ordinary worker.
sentences.translation: 지방정부 재정과 국유기업, 국가 벤처 투자 기금의 자금을 선호 부문으로 유도하면서 관료들이 '과도하고 파괴적인 경쟁을 방지'하고 기술 인력들에게는 '나라를 위해 봉사하려는 헌신의 감정을 함양'하도록 촉구하는 국가 주도 혁신은 전기차, 재생에너지, AI 분야에서 세계적으로 경쟁력 있는 기업들을 만들어냈지만, 대형 산업체들은 2014년 이후 2,300만 개의 일자리를 줄였으며, 이는 이 기술 민족주의 전략의 혜택이 평범한 노동자에게 결코 내려가지 않을 수도 있음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: State-guided innovation
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which channels money from ... into favoured sectors
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절

grammarElements.text: has produced globally competitive firms
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사 (성과)

grammarElements.text: yet big industrial firms have shed 23 million jobs
grammarElements.label: yet 대비절

grammarElements.text: suggesting that ... may never trickle down
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결론)

vocabulary.example: The manufacturing sector has shed thousands of jobs due to automation.

expressions.usage: 부나 혜택이 상위에서 하위로 서서히 퍼진다는 경제 이론 관련 표현

patterns.pattern: [Strategy] has produced [positive results], yet [negative consequence], suggesting that [critical assessment]
patterns.explanation: 전략의 성과와 부작용을 병치시킨 뒤 비판적 평가를 이끌어내는 패턴
examples: Globalisation has produced unprecedented wealth, yet inequality has widened, suggesting that the gains were never fairly shared.
The war on drugs has produced record seizures, yet addiction rates have risen, suggesting that enforcement alone cannot solve the problem.

drillQuestions.question: Big industrial firms have ______ 23 million jobs since 2014.

sentences.notes: 인용부호로 감싼 관료적 표현들(prevent excessive competition, cultivate feelings of devotion)이 국가 주도 혁신의 이데올로기적 성격을 드러낸다. trickle down은 레이건 시대 경제학 비판에서 유래한 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: State-guided innovation
has produced globally competitive firms
yet big industrial firms have shed 23 million jobs
may never trickle down to the ordinary worker
koreanAlignment.ko: 평범한 노동자에게 결코 내려가지 않을 수도 있다

sentences.original: The sentiment of a young woman in Guizhou -- who jokes with friends that the real distinction between successful people and everyone else lies not in exam scores or job titles but in "whose amniotic fluid they were formed in" -- captures a generational disillusionment so profound that it has spawned an entire vocabulary of resignation, from "lying flat" to "full-time children" to the bitter arithmetic of involution, where effort might take you from 1 to 1.2 in a system that once promised leaps from 1 to 100.
sentences.translation: 성공한 사람들과 나머지 사이의 진짜 차이는 시험 점수나 직함이 아니라 '누구의 양수 속에서 만들어졌느냐'에 있다고 친구들과 농담하는 구이저우의 한 젊은 여성의 심정은, 너무도 깊은 세대적 환멸을 포착하고 있어서 그것이 '탕핑(누워 있기)'에서 '전업 자녀'까지, 그리고 한때 1에서 100으로의 도약을 약속했던 시스템에서 노력이 기껏해야 1에서 1.2로만 데려다주는 내권(인볼루션)의 씁쓸한 산술에 이르기까지, 체념의 어휘 전체를 탄생시켰다.

grammarElements.text: The sentiment of a young woman in Guizhou
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: who jokes with friends that ...
grammarElements.label: 관계절

grammarElements.text: captures a generational disillusionment so profound that
grammarElements.label: 본동사 + so ... that 결과 구문

grammarElements.text: it has spawned an entire vocabulary of resignation
grammarElements.label: 결과절

grammarElements.text: where effort might take you from 1 to 1.2
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (장소적)

vocabulary.example: Growing disillusionment with the political system is driving young people away from the polls.
The crisis spawned a wave of protest movements across the continent.

expressions.usage: 경쟁과 노력을 거부하는 중국 청년 세대의 저항적 생활 태도를 가리키는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Individual voice] captures a [collective feeling] so profound that it has spawned [cultural phenomenon]
patterns.explanation: 개인의 목소리가 세대적 감정을 대변하며 문화 현상으로 이어졌음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The viral post captures a frustration so profound that it has spawned an entire genre of memes.
The novelist's protagonist captures an alienation so profound that it has spawned academic conferences on modern loneliness.

drillQuestions.question: The sentiment captures a generational disillusionment so profound that it has ______ an entire vocabulary of resignation.

sentences.notes: 양수(amniotic fluid)라는 생물학적 표현으로 태생적 불평등을 비유하는 것이 충격적이다. 1에서 1.2라는 산술적 비유가 노력 대비 보상의 처참한 비율을 직관적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The sentiment of a young woman in Guizhou
whose amniotic fluid they were formed in
captures a generational disillusionment so profound
an entire vocabulary of resignation
effort might take you from 1 to 1.2

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: anecdote as epitome (개인의 일화로 세대 전체를 대표)
